settingsDatabase.title: Database Management
settingsDatabase.dangerBody: These actions are irreversible and will delete data.
settingsDatabase.purgeDescription: Purge all content data to start fresh. This will delete all movies, series, episodes, embeddings, watch history, ratings, recommendations, taste profiles, and assistant conversations. Library configuration and user accounts are preserved.
settingsDatabase.irreversibleBody: You are about to permanently delete ALL content from your database. This includes:

settingsApiKeys.title: API Keys
settingsApiKeys.subtitle: API keys allow programmatic access to the Aperture API. Keys are shown only once when created.
settingsApiKeys.empty: No API keys found. Create your first API key to enable programmatic access.
settingsApiKeys.keyNameHelper: A descriptive name to identify this key
settingsApiKeys.createdWarningBody: Copy this key now. You won't be able to see it again!
settingsApiKeys.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke the API key “{{name}}”?
settingsApiKeys.revokeWarning: This action cannot be undone. Any applications using this key will lose access.

settingsBackup.title: Database Backup & Restore
settingsBackup.backupsToRetainHelper: Number of backup files to keep (older backups are automatically deleted)
settingsBackup.scheduleNote: Automatic backups run daily at 2:00 AM
settingsBackup.noBackups: No backups found. Create your first backup using the button above.
settingsBackup.restoreWarningBody: This will replace all current data with the backup. A pre-restore backup will be created automatically.
settingsBackup.restoreFrom: You are about to restore from: {{filename}}
settingsBackup.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please refresh and try again.

settingsTmdb.title: TMDb Integration
settingsTmdb.description: Enable TMDb integration for keywords, collections/franchises, and expanded crew data.
settingsTmdb.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.

settingsTrakt.title: Trakt Integration
settingsTrakt.description: Enable Trakt integration to let users sync their ratings.
settingsTrakt.registerSuffix: to get your Client ID and Secret.
settingsTrakt.helperSecretSaved: Client secret is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsTrakt.redirectHelper: Copy this URL to your Trakt app's Redirect URI field

settingsOmdb.title: OMDb Integration
settingsOmdb.description: Enable OMDb integration for Rotten Tomatoes scores, Metacritic ratings, and awards data.
settingsOmdb.descriptionSuffix: (1,000 requests/day free, or 100,000/day for paid subscribers).
settingsOmdb.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsOmdb.paidCaptionOff: Enable if you have a paid OMDb key for faster enrichment

settingsMdblist.title: MDBList Integration
settingsMdblist.description: Enable MDBList integration for internet popularity rankings in Top Picks, plus Letterboxd ratings, streaming availability, and additional keywords for metadata enrichment.
settingsMdblist.descriptionSuffix: (1,000 requests/day free, or higher limits for supporters).
settingsMdblist.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsMdblist.supporterCaptionOn: Using faster rate limits for supporters
settingsMdblist.supporterCaptionOff: Enable if you are an MDBList supporter for faster enrichment

settingsSeerr.title: Seerr Integration
settingsSeerr.description: Enable Seerr integration to let users request missing content discovered by AI.
settingsSeerr.learnMoreSuffix: or get your API key from your Seerr settings.
settingsSeerr.urlHelper: The URL of your Seerr instance
settingsSeerr.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new one to replace it.
settingsSeerr.helperNewKey: Get your API key from Seerr → Settings → General

settingsMediaServer.title: Media Server
settingsMediaServer.serverUrlHelper: The base URL of your media server
settingsMediaServer.helperSaved: API key is saved. Enter a new key to update it.
settingsMediaServer.helperNew: Generate an API key in your media server admin settings
settingsMediaServer.fillFieldsBeforeTest: Please fill in all fields before testing
settingsMediaServer.helpDocker: Your media server must be accessible from this container. For Docker deployments, use the container name (for example {{exampleUrl}}) or the host IP address.
settingsMediaServer.allowPasswordlessHelper: Enable this if your media server users don't have passwords set
settingsMediaServer.securityWarningBody: Only enable this if your Aperture instance is on a private network with no internet exposure. If your server is accessible via reverse proxy or the internet, leave this disabled to prevent unauthorized access.

settingsFileLocations.title: File Locations
settingsFileLocations.subtitle: Configure where your media server sees Aperture's libraries and media files
settingsFileLocations.detectCaption: Automatically detect paths by comparing media server files with Aperture's mounts
settingsFileLocations.apertureLibrariesHelper: Where your media server sees Aperture's output (the folder mounted as /aperture-libraries in Aperture)
settingsFileLocations.mediaServerPathPrefixHelper: Base path where your media server sees your media files (used for symlinks)
settingsFileLocations.detectSuccess: Paths detected! Click "Save Changes" to apply.

settingsLibraryConfig.title: Library Configuration
settingsLibraryConfig.subtitle: Select which libraries to include when syncing content
settingsLibraryConfig.syncTooltip: Refresh library list from media server
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesMovie: No movie libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.noLibrariesTv: No TV show libraries found. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.
settingsLibraryConfig.warningNoneEnabled: ⚠️ No libraries enabled — {{type}} sync will be skipped
settingsLibraryConfig.footerNextSync: Changes take effect on the next {{type}} sync. Run the appropriate sync job after making changes.
settingsLibraryConfig.emptyState: No libraries configured yet. Click "Sync from Media Server" to fetch your libraries.

settingsOutputFormat.title: AI Recommendations Output Format
settingsOutputFormat.subtitle: Choose how AI recommendation files are created for the media server
settingsOutputFormat.loadFailed: Failed to load output format configuration
settingsOutputFormat.moviesSymlinkHelp: Creates symbolic links to original movie files
settingsOutputFormat.moviesStrmHelp: Creates .strm files with paths or streaming URLs
settingsOutputFormat.seriesSymlinkHelp: Creates symlinks to original season folders (recommended)
settingsOutputFormat.seriesStrmRecommendation: Symlinks are recommended for TV series for better performance
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksIntro: Creates symbolic links pointing to your original media files. Faster playback, same quality.
settingsOutputFormat.explainSymlinksRequires: Both Aperture and your media server must access the same filesystem paths.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmIntro: Creates .strm files containing paths or streaming URLs. Works in all network configurations.
settingsOutputFormat.explainStrmBestFor: Docker setups, different machines, or when paths differ between systems.
settingsOutputFormat.symlinkWarningBody: Symlinks require that Aperture can access your media files at the exact same paths that your media server uses. If you're running Aperture in Docker, ensure the volume mounts match.

settingsMdblistSelector.manualPlaceholder: Enter list ID or paste MDBList URL…
settingsMdblistSelector.manualInfoSuffix: and paste the URL or ID here.
settingsMdblistSelector.emptyMyLists: You don't have any lists on MDBList
settingsMdblistSelector.emptySearchShort: Type at least 2 characters to search
settingsMdblistSelector.errInvalidId: Please enter a valid list ID or MDBList URL

settingsAiSetup.pageSubtitle: Configure AI providers for different features. Aperture recommends using OpenAI for all four functions, but feel free to explore other providers and models.
settingsAiSetup.cardEmbeddingsDesc: Converts text to vectors for semantic search and recommendations. Higher dimensions = better quality.
settingsAiSetup.cardChatDesc: Powers the AI assistant. Needs tool calling to search your library and make recommendations.
settingsAiSetup.cardTextGenDesc: Generates recommendation explanations, taste profiles, and playlist descriptions.
settingsAiSetup.cardExplorationDesc: Powers the Explore page. Uses AI to find meaningful connections from conceptual searches like "feel-good comedies".
settingsAiSetup.embeddingSetsBody: You have embeddings stored from multiple models. Inactive sets can be deleted to free up storage. When you switch back to a model with existing embeddings, they will be used immediately without regenerating.
settingsAiSetup.confirmDeleteSet: Delete all embeddings for "{{model}}"? This cannot be undone.

settingsAiExplanation.loadFailed: Failed to load AI explanation configuration
settingsAiExplanation.title: AI Explanation in Recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.subtitle: Control whether AI-generated explanations ("Why Aperture picked this for you") appear in recommendation descriptions
settingsAiExplanation.includeDesc: When enabled, each recommendation includes a personalized explanation of why it was selected
settingsAiExplanation.overrideDesc: When enabled, administrators can grant individual users the ability to toggle AI explanations for their own recommendations
settingsAiExplanation.infoThreeTierBody: Global default → Admin grants override per user → User chooses their preference.
settingsAiExplanation.infoGrantPath: To grant a user override permission, go to Users → select a user → enable "Allow AI explanation override".
